steps.title: Pin down the student's goal
steps.content: The task is to connect Save Me to “the history of neon in art.” The relevant note is: “Neon signs became popular with artists in the 1960s and continue to be popular today.” The correct choice must (1) mention Steele’s sculpture and (2) place it within the artistic tradition of neon, citing that 1960s-to-present timeline.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Neon signs like Save Me are popular with advertisers and artists alike…’: Mixes advertising into the goal, which is about art history specifically. Also emphasizes the handmade production note, which is off-task. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘With Save Me, Steele joins the decades-long lineage of artists who have helped make neon a popular artistic medium since the 1960s.’: Names the sculpture, places Steele in the artistic tradition of neon, and cites the 1960s starting point from the notes. Directly accomplishes the stated goal. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Continuing from the 1920s to today, neon lighting has been a popular material, both as an advertising tool and an artistic medium.’: Does not mention Steele or Save Me, and conflates advertising with art. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The pink neon sign Save Me nods to the rich history of a material whose artistic popularity has long been in decline.’: Contradicts the notes, which say neon’s artistic popularity “continues to be popular today.” Factually wrong. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
